concerns.text: Task T037 implements a permutation test by shuffling 'outcome vectors' (mean vividness) relative to the predictor. This violates the spec's FR-007 and SC-003 which require establishing a null distribution for the mixed-effects model. Shuffling aggregated means destroys the hierarchical structure (User-Track pairs) and the random intercept logic defined in the Plan, rendering the statistical test invalid and the FR untestable as implemented.

Task T028 implements SC-004 (Match Rate ≥ 80%) as a hard pipeline halt ('RAISE EXCEPTION'). This violates the spec's 'Independent Test' criteria for US-2 which requires the system to 'log unmatched cues' and proceed to aggregation for the matched subset. A hard halt prevents the sensitivity analysis (FR-006) and modeling (US-3) from running on the valid subset, effectively blocking the research if the threshold is narrowly missed.

Task T036 implements FR-006 (Sensitivity Analysis) by re-running matching and modeling but omits the re-aggregation step (T026) for thresholds 2 and 6. Since changing the matching threshold alters the set of matched cues and thus the composition of the 'User-Track Pairs' (Plan Summary), skipping re-aggregation makes the sensitivity analysis statistically invalid as it compares models on inconsistent data structures.

Task T015 implements a 'Minimum Listen Threshold' filter before the fallback logic in T017. The spec's Edge Cases mandate a fallback to 'global exposure' if >50% of users lack birth years. If the dataset is dominated by low-frequency tracks, T015 may filter the entire dataset to empty before T017 executes, causing the pipeline to crash or return null results instead of triggering the required safety fallback.

Tasks T016a and T026a update the spec to change the unit of analysis to 'User-Track Pair' but fail to update FR-007 (Permutation Test) to reflect this change. FR-007 currently describes shuffling 'memory outcomes' (implying individual instances). The tasks implement a method (shuffling means) that is mathematically incompatible with the original FR definition, leaving FR-007 untestable without a spec amendment.
